figures.caption: TABLE I. Sample-supported expressibility regimes. The boundary is governed by the statistical radius ϵstat(G,M) ∼

TABLE II. Conceptual difference between the realizable bounded-complexity learning law and the agnostic-adaptive Occam principle developed here. The realizable law is the base theorem; the new QML content comes from treating circuit complexity as an unknown, approximate, and sample-supported quantity.

FIG. 1. Illustrative approximation-estimation tradeoff for M = 5.0× 104. The dashed curves are stylized approximation errors dG, the solid curves add the statistical radius √ G/M , and the dotted vertical line marks Mε2 = 2.0× 103 for ε = 0.20.

FIG. 2. Sample-supported expressibility boundary for n = 20. The curves show Gsupported = min{2n,Mε2} for several target accuracies, ignoring logarithmic factors. The horizontal dotted line is the tomography saturation 220.
